reviews.review_msg: Making fair lab exams is not something Reid is good at. Man can go

Reid Staples is the best example of a professor picking favorites in the class and completely disrespecting the students he does not like. Avoid Reid because your grade is the hands of his discrimination.

To that one person that keeps writing bad reviews for one of the best professors in the department... I feel very sorry for you. Reid teaches some of the most complicated topics in Earth Science and the way he teaches is incredible. He is kind, funny, and really wants his students to succeed. Taking a class with Reid is an absolute privilege.
Provides zero material outside of his unreadable lecture slides designed to confuse students. Avoid Reid and take the course with another professor. Complete failure of SFU to hire this guy
very bad at lecturing. and offers no help, just rude words

You ask him a question and he looks at you in annoyance, what kind of professorOffers no help in office hours and picks favourite students. if you are not his favourite student be prepared to learn everything on your own. 0 out of 10 professor

In response to the student leaving all of these wack reviews for sfu easc profs, don't let that defer you from taking a class with Reid! He is genuinely the BEST instructor to have and is super passionate about what he teaches. Ask any student in easc and they will say the same. He is super approachable and eager to go in depth about concepts.
Reid is such an amazing prof! The one stars on here are not reflective of him or his teaching style at all!! Would take every earth science class with him if I could!

A hard class made worse by Reid's extremely bad grade allocation. Barely getting by with a C- should not happen when you put in hours of effort each week only to find out his exams contain completely unfair questions and the grading scheme involves examinations only.

Ignore the reviews slandering an amazing prof due to students' own faults. I didn't do too well in this class in the end, and still recognize what a wonderful prof Reid is. Absolute gem. Makes uninteresting content (to me) fun. Really wants you to succeed. Hardest part of this class is that your grade is 100% based on 5 exams: 3 lab, 2 lecture.

I love this guy. He is extremely approachable to the point where I want him to be my legal guardian, he is clear about his expectations, and his exams and homework are beyond fair. I can't even imagine not loving this guy, you can ask him the stupidest question and he will be gleeful to help you. Genuinely cares so much for his students. Groovy?

As someone who took both 202 and 206 this semester, I am appalled to see the comments that this specific student is leaving for all of the easc professors. Reid has been BY FAR the best prof I've had at sfu, and I very much look forward to future classes with him! He is very groovy, don't let the nasty comments misdirect you.

Having taken multiple EASC courses with Reid, I can say that he is one of (if not) my favourite lecturers in the department. He is genuinely passionate about teaching and cares about each of his students. If you put in the work, there is no reason you can't succeed in Reid's classes.
Needs to lecture better, every lecture is hard to understand and nobody can do well on the exams because of it.
Field 1 was confusing at best, numerous times I was lost. The course needs far better delivery

Mineralogy is a hard course and Reid acknowledges it but he needs to do a far better job providing help to students. He gives literally no information about what the lab exams are like other than"memorize these minerals". When the lab exams come you literally have no choice but to guess even when you've studied everything.also reads slides too much

There is a disgruntled student spamming this site and slandering instructors in the EASC department.

While the department may have high standards, every instructor works hard for their students' success. They are approachable and fair. Reid cares deeply about the students and goes above and beyond.

Great lecturer! Reid often teaches challenging courses, but includes review so students can remember prerequisite knowledge. Also great at breaking down complex topics into understandable information. Highly recommend taking his courses.
